info.title: Unified Software Commercialization Platform Public API
info.description: Contract-first public client and admin API. Product, Application, Tenant and User contexts are resolved from verified sessions; identifiers supplied by clients are never authorization facts.

get.summary: Read process liveness
Read dependency readiness

post.summary: Create a versioned pre-product software blueprint
post.description: The blueprint precedes Product creation and is authorized in platform scope. The document selects every target Application, exact available package/template versions, delivery modes, secret references and controlled output root; it cannot submit catalog readiness or secret values.

get.summary: List server-authorized redacted assembly output targets
get.description: Platform-scope planning read. The response is filtered by the required Assembly environment and never exposes source roots, artifact roots or host paths. A missing default remains null; clients must not infer the first item as default.

get.summary: List ordinary available software creation options
get.description: Platform-scope planning read fixed to the ordinary catalog. An empty production catalog returns stable empty arrays. Clients cannot select catalog scope through query, headers or Blueprint fields.

get.summary: List explicitly authorized experimental software creation options
get.description: Separate platform-scope route fixed to the experimental verified catalog. It requires assembly.experimental.use and cannot be enabled by ordinary requests or client-supplied scope.

get.summary: Read the current version of a pre-product blueprint

post.summary: Resolve an immutable multi-Application assembly plan
post.description: The server resolves and pins the exact catalog snapshot, packages, every Application's target/template/delivery mode, SDKs, dependencies, risks and expected outputs. Clients cannot submit a plan document.

post.summary: Confirm a locked plan and start idempotent software assembly
post.description: High-risk platform-scope operation. The server verifies the plan version, plan checksum and exact confirmation summary before starting. output_target_ref is a server-controlled workspace reference, never a client filesystem path.

get.summary: Read a locked assembly plan and confirmation state
List durable assembly runs in stable reverse chronological order
Read resumable assembly execution status and artifact references

post.summary: Create a durable linked attempt for a retryable failed assembly run
Cancel an unclaimed planned assembly run
post.description: High-risk platform-scope lifecycle operation. Cancellation succeeds only while the durable dispatch remains unclaimed and the Run is still planned; executing Runs must reach a recoverable terminal state instead of being killed mid-transaction.

get.summary: Read an immutable assembly manifest
get.description: Platform-scope recovery read for an initial completed Run or a completed lifecycle successor. The canonical document contains only locked artifact metadata and secret references, never secret values.

get.summary: Read the immutable generated-project ownership and checksum lock
get.description: Platform-scope recovery read used for deterministic regeneration, conflict detection and rollback planning.

get.summary: Read the current immutable lifecycle artifact head
get.description: Returns only the server-resolved current Manifest and lock identity/checksums for optimistic lifecycle planning. It never exposes workspace or artifact-store paths.

post.summary: Create an immutable server-verified assembly upgrade plan
post.description: The server reloads the current Manifest, lock, source plan, catalog scope and authorized workspace. Client checksums are optimistic concurrency guards, never replacement documents or filesystem facts.

post.summary: Create an immutable server-verified eject ownership plan
post.description: Only safe relative generated or integration paths in the current lock may be selected. Eject changes ownership to forked without modifying file contents and stops automatic overwrite.

get.summary: Read a browser-safe immutable assembly lifecycle plan

post.summary: Start a durable locked lifecycle operation
post.description: High-risk platform-scope operation requiring recent authentication. The Plan, source artifacts, catalog and workspace snapshot are revalidated before an Operation and dispatch are committed atomically.

get.summary: Read durable lifecycle operation state and safe recovery evidence

post.summary: Cancel an unclaimed planned lifecycle operation
post.description: High-risk platform-scope operation requiring recent authentication. Executing operations cannot be killed mid-transaction and must use a later rollback when available.

post.summary: Start a durable rollback from server-owned lifecycle evidence
post.description: High-risk platform-scope operation requiring recent authentication. The request never accepts artifact paths; the server reloads the predecessor Manifest, lock, rollback point and journal and stops on any drift.

get.summary: List products visible to the administrator

post.summary: Create a product and its official tenant

get.summary: Read one authorized product

post.summary: Authenticate a registered client and resolve trusted Product/Application/Tenant context
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Client proof and request nonce provide replay control; successful retries may return an equivalent short-lived session.

get.summary: Read the current trusted capability set for an authorized product

put.summary: Replace the versioned capability set for an authorized product
put.x-idempotency-exemption: PUT is naturally idempotent and uses the expected_version precondition.

get.summary: List technical applications under an authorized product

post.summary: Create a technical application under a product

put.summary: Replace exact redirect, origin, deep-link, and named authentication return-target allowlists
put.x-idempotency-exemption: PUT replaces a complete versioned allowlist and is naturally idempotent.

post.summary: Suspend an application without deleting history
Register a client identity and deliver its initial proof once
Rotate a client proof and deliver the new secret once
Revoke a client proof and its active client sessions

get.summary: List official and agent tenants under an authorized product

post.summary: Create an agent tenant under an authorized product
Bind a global user as an administrator in one tenant scope
Register a global user inside the trusted client context

get.summary: Resolve the current user and trusted access decision

post.summary: Start a non-enumerating password recovery challenge
Consume a one-time recovery proof and replace the password
Authenticate a user inside the trusted client context
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Authentication attempts are rate-limited security events and are not business mutations.

post.summary: Start a server-bound external identity flow
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Each call intentionally creates a short-lived one-time state/nonce flow.

post.summary: Consume a provider callback for a server-bound external identity flow
post.x-idempotency-exemption: The client-session and state-bound provider callback is a one-time authentication attempt; replay is rejected by the flow state machine.

post.summary: Request a non-enumerating registration verification delivery
Exchange a one-time WeChat code using the bound Application configuration
post.x-idempotency-exemption: The client-session and state-bound WeChat code is a one-time authentication attempt; replay is rejected by the flow state machine.

get.summary: Read the current user's profile

patch.summary: Update the current user's profile with optimistic concurrency

put.summary: Change the password after recent authentication

get.summary: List redacted sessions for the current user

delete.summary: Revoke one session owned by the current user

get.summary: Read a non-authoritative explanation of current identity and scoped admission state
get.description: This projection is for account UI only. Protected operations independently run the Account Access Decision Workflow with a server-owned operation policy; clients cannot use this response as authorization.

get.summary: List redacted external identities linked to the current user

post.summary: Link a verified external identity to the current user

delete.summary: Unlink an external identity after recent authentication

post.summary: Rotate a refresh token and issue a new token pair
post.description: client_request_id is the narrow retry key. Repeating the same request after a lost response returns the same replacement result during the recovery window; presenting the old token under a different request id is replay and revokes the family.
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Refresh-token rotation has dedicated concurrency and replay semantics.

post.summary: Revoke the current session
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Revoking an already-revoked current session is a stable no-op.

get.summary: List redacted users within the administrator's authorized scope
get.description: This global collection requires platform scope. Product and tenant administrators must use their scoped workspace collection.

get.summary: List redacted users admitted to the authorized product workspace
List redacted users admitted to the authorized tenant workspace

put.summary: Change global user security status with platform-scoped high-risk permission
Change product admission without changing global identity or entitlement
Change tenant admission without changing product admission, global identity, or entitlement

post.summary: Authenticate an administrator and create a server-side management session
post.description: Cookie is the browser default. Bearer additionally requires a valid pre-registered controlled_client proof; the global emergency switch alone never authorizes issuance.
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Authentication attempts are rate-limited security events and are not business mutations.

get.summary: Resolve the current administrator identity and fresh authorization snapshot

post.summary: Rotate the one-time administrator refresh token family
post.description: Cookie mode is the access-expiry recovery path: it reads only the HttpOnly SameSite refresh cookie and requires exact Origin, but not an in-memory CSRF token. Bearer mode requires refresh_token and the exact controlled client + credential proof bound at login. The current authorization snapshot is resolved before consuming the old token, while the final rotation still revalidates token state under a transaction lock. Any reuse of an old refresh token revokes the whole family.
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Refresh-token rotation has dedicated narrow concurrency and replay semantics.

post.summary: Revoke the administrator token family and clear management cookies
post.description: Cookie mode validates an active access cookie with its CSRF token, or falls back to a valid refresh cookie from the same session/family only when access is expired or absent. Proof validation, family revocation, and the security outbox commit atomically. Transient lookup or outbox failures never bypass validation and remain retryable.
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Revoking an already-revoked token family is a stable no-op.

post.summary: Evaluate current product/tenant entitlement using server time
Grant entitlement from an audited source

get.summary: List redacted devices in the resolved scope

post.summary: Atomically check entitlement/device limit and bind an installation
post.x-idempotency-exemption: client_request_id and installation key identity are the module-defined idempotency controls.

post.summary: Prove installation possession and refresh online/offline lease state
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Server nonce is single-use; retries return a stable result without extending twice.

post.summary: Revoke a device binding in the current scope
Generate a scoped activation-code batch
Create a short-lived one-time delivery artifact
Suspend a batch without revoking historical grants
Claim an activation code and start recoverable entitlement orchestration
post.x-idempotency-exemption: product_id plus client_request_id is the module-defined idempotency key.

get.summary: Read a redacted redemption result in the current scope
List currently sellable offers and server-selected prices

post.summary: Create an offer and draft version
Publish immutable offer and price versions
Create an order from a server-issued catalog snapshot

get.summary: List orders for the current Product/Tenant/User scope
Read one order in the current scope

post.summary: Cancel a still-unpaid order through its state machine
Create a payment intent from the server-side payable order summary
Create short-lived safe payment-launch parameters
Start an audited asynchronous refund
Invoke a versioned logical model through the AI Gateway
post.description: The server resolves Provider, provider model, credentials, route version and price version. For SSE, exactly one terminal event reports completed, cancelled or failed settlement state.
post.x-idempotency-exemption: product scope plus client_request_id is the AI/Usage idempotency boundary and prevents duplicate settlement.

put.summary: Create an immutable logical-model route version

post.summary: Create an immutable metering price version

get.summary: List metered usage and safe billing details in the resolved scope

post.summary: Create a trusted hosted.auth or hosted.account interaction

get.summary: Recover a redacted hosted interaction projection

post.summary: Open or rotate the short-lived hosted browser session
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Each valid interaction launch intentionally rotates the active browser session and revokes the old cookie.

post.summary: Authenticate inside hosted.auth and issue a one-time completion code
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Credential attempts are rate-limited security events and never create two grants for one interaction.

post.summary: Complete an authenticated hosted.account interaction
Cancel a non-terminal hosted interaction
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Cancellation is a terminal idempotent state transition for the authenticated browser session.

post.summary: Exchange the one-time completion code from the original trusted client scope
post.x-idempotency-exemption: The short-lived completion code is the single-use grant; replay is rejected by the lease-backed grant state machine.

put.summary: Create or replace a scoped administrator role binding

get.summary: Search redacted audit events inside the authorized scope

ClientSessionBearer.description: Short-lived client session containing server-resolved ProductContext, ApplicationContext and TenantContext.

UserBearer.description: User session bound to trusted Product/Application and server-resolved Tenant context.

HostedSessionCookie.name: __Host-platform_hosted_session
HostedSessionCookie.description: Short-lived Hosted UI browser session. Server sets Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Path=/ and no Domain. Unsafe methods also require exact Hosted Origin and X-CSRF-Token.

AdminAccessCookie.name: __Host-platform_admin_access
AdminAccessCookie.description: Default browser administrator access. Server sets Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, Path=/ and no Domain. Unsafe methods also require exact Origin and X-CSRF-Token.

AdminRefreshCookie.name: __Secure-platform_admin_refresh
AdminRefreshCookie.description: Rotating browser refresh proof. Server sets Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, Path=/api/v1/admin/auth and no Domain; accepted only by admin refresh/logout.

AdminBearer.description: Optional non-browser administrator transport explicitly issued to controlled CLI/automation; every target product/tenant is re-authorized by Access Control.

DeveloperKey.description: Restricted developer key. The key resolves Product/Tenant/scope server-side and cannot select arbitrary contexts.

RequestId.name: X-Request-Id
RequestId.description: Optional caller correlation ID; not an authorization or idempotency fact.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Opaque operation-scoped idempotency key. Reuse with a different payload returns conflict.

AdminCsrfToken.name: X-CSRF-Token
AdminCsrfToken.description: Required with exact Origin for unsafe requests authenticated by an administrator access Cookie, except refresh which recovers after access expiry using exact Origin plus SameSite refresh Cookie. Bound to the server session and rotated with it. Omit for Admin Bearer.

HostedCsrfToken.name: X-CSRF-Token
HostedCsrfToken.description: Required with exact Hosted UI Origin for every unsafe request authenticated by HostedSessionCookie.

HostedInteractionId.name: interaction_id
HostedInteractionId.description: Opaque high-entropy HostedInteraction identifier; never selects Product/Application/Tenant by itself.

ProductId.name: product_id
ProductId.description: Target resource only; access is independently authorized from the admin session.

TenantId.name: tenant_id
TenantId.description: Target resource only; access is independently authorized from the admin session.

BlueprintId.name: blueprint_id
BlueprintId.description: Pre-product blueprint identifier. It is a resource locator, never a Product authorization fact.

HealthReady.description: Dependency readiness; status code is 503 when any required dependency is unavailable

CapabilitySetProjection.description: Current trusted capability set, or null when the real product has no set yet

ClientCredential.description: Client registration or rotated proof. `secret` is returned once and never appears in later reads.

AssemblyOutputTargetCatalog.description: Server-authorized output targets with redacted display metadata only

AssemblyCatalogOptions.description: Server-projected software creation options with stable ordering and no paths, checksums, execution descriptors or readiness injection fields

AssemblyBlueprint.description: Versioned pre-product blueprint and its canonical machine document

AssemblyPlan.description: Immutable server-resolved assembly plan. The document locks all selected Applications and their exact templates/delivery modes.

AssemblyRun.description: Resumable assembly execution state and controlled artifact references

AssemblyRunPage.description: Stable cursor page of durable assembly runs

AssemblyManifest.description: Immutable initial or lifecycle-successor assembly manifest with canonical document, exclusive provenance, and independently stored checksums

GeneratedProjectLock.description: Immutable generated-project lock with source ownership and baseline checksums

AssemblyLifecycleArtifactState.description: Current server-resolved lifecycle artifact head without paths or documents

AssemblyLifecyclePlan.description: Immutable server-verified lifecycle analysis with browser-safe diffs, conflicts and rollback policy

AssemblyLifecycleOperation.description: Durable lifecycle execution state and safe recovery evidence

IssuedUserSession.description: Newly issued user session; credentials are returned only from authentication mutations

CurrentUserSession.description: Current user session projection without access or refresh credentials

UserSessionPage.description: Redacted sessions owned by the current user

ProductUserAccessDecision.description: Trusted global, product, tenant, then entitlement access decision

UserAccessMutation.description: Audited identity or scoped admission mutation

AdminSession.description: Administrator identity, current server authorization snapshot and session expiry. Cookie transport sets both management cookies; bearer transport returns token_pair. Responses must use Cache-Control: no-store.

Set-Cookie.description: Cookie transport may emit separate access and rotating refresh Set-Cookie headers with the attributes fixed by the Identity contract.

AdminLogout.description: Administrator token family revoked. Cookie transport expires both cookies using their original Path and security attributes.

Set-Cookie.description: Separate expired access and refresh cookies.

ExternalIdentityPage.description: Redacted linked identities for the current user

HostedInteractionLaunch.description: Short-lived Hosted UI launch containing only the opaque interaction identifier in its URL

HostedBrowserSession.description: Opened Hosted browser session. The opaque session is set only as a secure HttpOnly cookie; csrf_token stays in browser memory.

Set-Cookie.description: __Host-platform_hosted_session with Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Path=/ and no Domain.

HostedCompletion.description: Safe registered return URL carrying only code, state and interaction_id

HostedExchange.description: One-time hosted completion exchange. Tokens are returned only for authorization_code grants.

AiResponse.description: Final JSON response or an SSE stream with one terminal event

schema.description: SSE events use versioned JSON data; the terminal event contains request_id, terminal status and usage_settlement_ref.

output_target_ref.description: Opaque server-controlled workspace reference; absolute, relative and platform filesystem paths are not accepted.

environments.description: Server-projected, deduplicated and stably sorted recovery environments from validated applications.

confirmation_checksum.description: Server-revalidated confirmation summary checksum for recovery and execution.

output_target_ref.description: Opaque server-controlled workspace reference selected for this run.

manifest_url.description: Same-origin management API URL for the immutable manifest.

lock_url.description: Same-origin management API URL for the generated-project lock.

continuation_id.description: Always returned from the same opaque namespace, including when no account exists.

current_credential.description: Re-authenticates the current user; recent authentication is evaluated from the trusted server session.

expected_version.description: Use 0 when no explicit scoped access fact exists.

continuation_id.description: Always returned from the same opaque namespace, including when the identifier is already registered.

audit_id.description: Present on the link mutation response; omitted from list projections.

return_url.description: Server-built registered return target with only code, state and interaction_id.

proof.description: At least 256 bits of random secret material, delivered once and never returned or logged.

transport.description: Browsers use cookie. Bearer is only a request intent and also requires controlled_client.

refresh_token.description: Required only for bearer transport; cookie transport rejects body token material.

session_version.description: Monotonically increases after each successful refresh; clients must ignore responses older than the newest version already observed.

controlled_client_id.description: Present only for bearer sessions; never includes a secret or digest.

csrf_token.description: Returned for cookie transport and kept only in frontend memory; null for bearer transport.

launch_parameters.description: Channel-minimized short-lived parameters; never contains reusable provider credentials.

max_budget_hint.description: Non-authoritative client ceiling hint in the product's configured quota unit.

rollout_scope.description: Authorized product/application rollout constraints; never credentials.

cost_amount_integer.description: Returned only when the current authorization scope may view internal cost.
